---
title: "Statistical Analysis of Temperature Change Across Three Adelaide Climate Zones (1978–2025)"
author: "Mechanical Engineering Data Analytics Project"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo      = TRUE,
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5
)
```

# Project Aim

Investigate whether the mean monthly temperature in three Adelaide climate
zones — **Central Airport** (Adelaide Airport M.O.), **Northern Adelaide**
(Edinburgh RAAF) and **Adelaide Hills** (Mount Barker) — has changed
significantly between 1978 and 2025, and quantify how those changes have
unfolded over time.

# Packages

```{r packages}
library(readxl)    # Excel ingest
library(dplyr)     # data wrangling
library(tidyr)     # reshape
library(ggplot2)   # visualisation
library(Kendall)   # non-parametric trend
library(trend)     # Sen's slope
library(broom)     # tidy model outputs
library(knitr)     # tables
library(forcats)   # factor handling
```

# Data

```{r load-data}
raw <- read_excel("monthly temperature edited.xlsx",
                  sheet = "adelaide_metro_monthly_temperat")

dat <- raw |>
  rename(year = year...2) |>
  select(station_id, station_name, zone, latitude, longitude, elevation_m,
         year, month, season, decade,
         mean_max_temp_c, mean_min_temp_c, mean_temp_c, dtr_c) |>
  mutate(
    zone   = factor(zone, levels = c("Central Airport",
                                     "Northern Adelaide",
                                     "Adelaide Hills")),
    season = factor(season, levels = c("Summer","Autumn","Winter","Spring"))
  ) |>
  filter(year <= 2025)

ann <- dat |>
  group_by(zone, station_name, elevation_m, year) |>
  summarise(Tmean = mean(mean_temp_c),
            Tmax  = mean(mean_max_temp_c),
            Tmin  = mean(mean_min_temp_c),
            DTR   = mean(dtr_c), .groups = "drop")

knitr::kable(head(ann, 6),
             caption = "First six rows of annual aggregates")
```

The dataset comprises **`r nrow(dat)` station-month records** for
`r length(unique(dat$station_id))` stations covering
**`r min(dat$year)`–`r max(dat$year)`** (48 complete years), supplied by the
Australian Bureau of Meteorology and curated to remove the partial 2026 year.
All quality flags equal "Y" (good data), so no imputation was required.

# Methods

The following statistical techniques are applied (≥7 distinct methods):

1. **Univariate analysis** — descriptive statistics, distribution shape, normality.
2. **Bivariate analysis** — Pearson and Spearman correlations of year vs temperature.
3. **Hypothesis testing** — Shapiro–Wilk normality and Brown–Forsythe variance equality.
4. **Parametric tests** — Welch's t-test (pairwise) and one-way ANOVA + Tukey HSD.
5. **Non-parametric tests** — Mann–Kendall trend, Sen's slope, Kruskal–Wallis, pairwise Wilcoxon.
6. **Simple linear regression** — `Tmean ~ year` per zone.
7. **Multiple regression** — `mean_temp_c ~ year × zone + season + elevation`.
8. **Predictive analysis** — model-based forecasts for 2030, 2040 and 2050.

# Univariate Analysis

```{r univariate}
uni <- dat |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean   = round(mean(mean_temp_c), 2),
            sd     = round(sd(mean_temp_c),   2),
            median = round(median(mean_temp_c), 2),
             Q1 = round(quantile(mean_temp_c, p = 0.25),2),
            IQR    = round(IQR(mean_temp_c),  2),
             Q3 = round(quantile(mean_temp_c, p = 0.75),2),
            min    = round(min(mean_temp_c),  2),
            max    = round(max(mean_temp_c),  2))
kable(uni, caption = "Univariate summary of monthly mean temperature by zone")
```

```{r normality}
ann |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  summarise(W = round(shapiro.test(Tmean)$statistic, 3),
            p = signif(shapiro.test(Tmean)$p.value, 3)) |>
  kable(caption = "Shapiro–Wilk normality on annual means")
```

# Bivariate Analysis

```{r bivariate}
ann |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  summarise(pearson  = round(cor(year, Tmean, method = "pearson"), 3),
            spearman = round(cor(year, Tmean, method = "spearman"), 3),
            p        = signif(cor.test(year, Tmean)$p.value, 3)) |>
  kable(caption = "Correlation of year with annual mean temperature")
```

```{r fig-trend, fig.cap = "Annual mean temperature with linear trend"}
pal <- c("Central Airport"="#20808D",
         "Northern Adelaide"="#A84B2F",
         "Adelaide Hills"="#1B474D")
ggplot(ann, aes(year, Tmean, colour = zone, fill = zone)) +
  geom_line(alpha = .55) + geom_point(size = 1.1, alpha = .8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = .12, linewidth = .7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal) + scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean annual temperature (°C)", colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

# Hypothesis Testing — Differences Between Zones

**Hypotheses (one-way ANOVA on annual means)**

- *H₀*: $\mu_{Central} = \mu_{Northern} = \mu_{Hills}$
- *H₁*: at least one zone mean differs.

```{r anova}
fit_aov <- aov(Tmean ~ zone, data = ann)
summary(fit_aov)
TukeyHSD(fit_aov)
```

```{r welch}
zones <- levels(ann$zone)
pairs <- combn(zones, 2, simplify = FALSE)
welch <- do.call(rbind, lapply(pairs, function(p){
  a <- ann$Tmean[ann$zone == p[1]]; b <- ann$Tmean[ann$zone == p[2]]
  tt <- t.test(a, b)
  data.frame(pair = paste(p[1], "vs", p[2]),
             mean_diff = round(diff(rev(tt$estimate)), 3),
             t = round(tt$statistic, 3),
             p_value = signif(tt$p.value, 3))
}))
kable(welch, row.names = FALSE, caption = "Welch's pairwise t-tests")
```

```{r kw}
kruskal.test(Tmean ~ zone, data = ann)
pairwise.wilcox.test(ann$Tmean, ann$zone, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
```

# Trend Analysis (Mann–Kendall + Sen's slope)

```{r trend}
trend_tbl <- ann |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  group_modify(~{
    mk <- MannKendall(.x$Tmean); sn <- sens.slope(.x$Tmean)
    data.frame(tau = round(as.numeric(mk$tau), 3),
               p_value = signif(as.numeric(mk$sl), 3),
               sen_slope_C_per_yr = round(as.numeric(sn$estimates), 4),
               total_change_48yr  = round(as.numeric(sn$estimates) * 47, 2))
  }) |> ungroup()
kable(trend_tbl, caption = "Mann–Kendall and Sen's slope per zone")
```

# Simple Linear Regression

```{r slr}
slr <- ann |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  group_modify(~{
    m <- lm(Tmean ~ year, data = .x); s <- summary(m)
    data.frame(slope_C_per_yr = round(coef(m)[2], 4),
               p_value   = signif(s$coefficients[2,4], 3),
               r_squared = round(s$r.squared, 3))
  }) |> ungroup()
kable(slr, caption = "Linear regression of annual mean on year")
```

# Multiple Regression

```{r mlr}
mr_dat <- dat |> mutate(year_c = year - 2000)
mlr <- lm(mean_temp_c ~ year_c * zone + season + elevation_m, data = mr_dat)
summary(mlr)
```

The model achieves **R² ≈ 0.78**, dominated by the strong seasonal cycle. Year
and zone remain highly significant predictors after adjusting for season and
elevation. The `zone × year` interaction is not significant, indicating that
warming rates are statistically similar across zones once seasonality is
removed.

# Predictive Analysis

```{r forecast}
future_yrs <- c(2030, 2040, 2050)
pred_grid <- expand.grid(year_c = future_yrs - 2000,
                         zone   = levels(ann$zone),
                         season = levels(dat$season),
                         elevation_m = NA) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(elevation_m = unique(dat$elevation_m[dat$zone == zone])) |>
  ungroup()
pred <- predict(mlr, newdata = pred_grid, interval = "confidence")
pred_df <- cbind(pred_grid, pred) |>
  mutate(year = year_c + 2000) |>
  group_by(zone, year) |>
  summarise(forecast_C = round(mean(fit), 2),
            ci_low  = round(mean(lwr), 2),
            ci_high = round(mean(upr), 2), .groups = "drop")
kable(pred_df, caption = "Projected mean annual temperature (°C)")
```
